one_time_description: Crown Indigenous Relations transfer of $7,597,537 received in 2022 with no comparable in prior year, likely a settlement or one-time reconciliation payment
economic_notes: Band operates Kanaka Bar Land and Resources Limited Partnership, Kanaka Bar Land and Resources Inc., and KBES Services LLP as government business enterprises. Also has interests in Kwoiek Creek Resources LP. Kanaka Necessities Store sales noted. BC Indigenous Clean Energy Initiative involvement. Contingent liability as covenantor on $3,100,000 term loan for Kwoiek Creek Resources Limited Partnership.
notes: Large one-time Crown Indigenous Relations transfer of $7,597,537 in 2022 (nil in 2021) dramatically increased revenue and surplus. Prior year comparative restated for deferred revenue correction of $33,703. Three government business enterprises accounted for on modified equity basis (Kanaka Bar Land and Resources LP, Kanaka Bar Land and Resources Inc., KBES Services LLP). Five segments fully consolidated. No remuneration schedule included in the financial statements. Total government transfers calculated as ISC + Crown Indigenous Relations + Province of BC + First Nations Health Authority + Government of Canada + CMHC = 1,273,450 + 7,597,537 + 1,754,081 + 572,552 + 352,339 + 39,108 = 11,589,067; adding BC Hydro 14,184 + New Relationship Trust 5,000 + United Way BC 20,000 + Real Estate Foundation of BC 50,000 = 11,678,251 used for total_government_transfers approximation. OSR includes taxation, management fees, interest, rental, store sales and other non-government sources. Term loan is repayable on demand (BMO $993,055) plus CMHC mortgage $9,813.
